variants.title: Filtrec FS170B7T125 Suction Strainer | Replaces the Mahle PI17107
variants.option1Value: Filtrec FS170B7T125 Suction Strainer | Replaces the Mahle PI17107
variants.description: Genuine Filtrec FS170B7T125. This Filtrec element is designed to meet or exceed the quality and performance of brand OEM’s ensuring superior filtration performance and long-lasting durability. Engineered with their high performance media and a durable design, the FS170B7T125 provides reliable and consistent performance over its lifespan, even in harsh operating environments. When you order from FilterFinder, you can trust that you are getting a reliable and high-quality replacement filter that will keep your system running smoothly.
specifications: Style: Suction Strainer, Media: Wire Mesh, Efficiency 1: 125µ, Outer Diameter: 102.0 mm, Length: 155.0 mm, Thread: 1 1/2" BSP, Δ Collapse Pressure: 1 bar, Temperature Range: -30 - +100°C, Efficiency 1 The efficiency of the element is shown using the Micron (µ) and Beta (β) ratings. The micron rating indicates the size of the particle being filtered and the beta rating the efficiency , calculated as 1/β. E.g. a rating of β200 will be 99.5% efficient whereas β1000 would be 99.9%.: 125µ, Media Type: Wire Mesh, Flow Direction: OUT-IN, Collapse Pressure: 1 BAR

variants.title: Fleetguard HF35160 Hydraulic Filters | Replaces the Mahle PI17107
variants.option1Value: Fleetguard HF35160 Hydraulic Filters | Replaces the Mahle PI17107
variants.description: Fleetguard® HF35160 hydraulic cartridge filter with wire mesh media is designed to help keep your precision hydraulic systems running as intended, providing a solution for various applications.  This filter meets or exceeds the equipment OEM quality and performance and is the first aftermarket replacement available on the market today, bringing your operation top-quality performance and an overall unbeatable total cost of ownership.

The micron rating indicates the size of the particle being filtered and the beta rating the efficiency , calculated as 1/β.
